title: Carla's Sandwich
author: Debbie Herman
description: A charming story about a girl named Carla who brings unique sandwiches to school, teaching her classmates to celebrate individuality.

sentences.englishOriginal: Welcome to Storyline Online brought to you by the SAG-AFTRA Foundation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歡迎來到由演員工會-美國電視廣播藝人聯合會基金會為您帶來的「線上故事劇场」。

phraseNotes.title: brought to you by
phraseNotes.explanation: 「由……為您帶來/贊助」，常用於節目、活動或影片開頭，介紹主辦或贊助單位。

examples.english: This podcast is brought to you by our generous listeners.
examples.chinese: 本播客節目是由我們慷慨的聽眾贊助播出的。

sentences.englishOriginal: I'm Allison Janney and today I will be reading "Carla's Sandwich" Written by Debbie Herman and Illustrated by Sheila Bailey.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是艾莉森·珍妮，今天我要讀的故事是《卡拉的 三明治》，作者是黛比·赫曼，插圖由席拉·貝利繪製。

vocabularyNotes.title: illustrate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「為（書等）畫插圖」。在書本封面上常用過去分詞形式 illustrated by 表示「由……繪製插圖」。

examples.english: She loves to read children's books illustrated by famous artists.
examples.chinese: 她喜歡閱讀由著名藝術家繪製插圖的童書。

sentences.englishOriginal: Carla brought weird sandwiches to school.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 卡拉總是帶一些奇特的三明治去學校。

vocabularyNotes.title: weird
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「怪異的、古怪的、奇特的」，口語中非常常用。

examples.english: I heard a weird noise coming from the backyard last night.
examples.chinese: 昨天晚上我聽見後院傳來一陣奇怪的聲音。

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster noticed it first.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特是最先注意到這件事的人。

vocabularyNotes.title: notice
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「注意到、察覺到」。

examples.english: Did you notice that she changed her hair color?
examples.chinese: 你有注意到她染了頭髮嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: He was sitting next to Carla at lunch one Monday.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在某個星期一吃午餐時，他正好坐在卡拉旁邊。

phraseNotes.title: next to
phraseNotes.explanation: 介系詞片語「在……旁邊、緊鄰」。

examples.english: The library is located right next to the bank.
examples.chinese: 圖書館就位於銀行旁邊。

grammarNotes.title: Past Continuous Tense (過去進行式)
grammarNotes.explanation: 使用「was/were + 現在分詞 (V-ing)」結構，用來描寫過去某個特定時間點正在發生的動作或故事背景。

examples.english: It was raining heavily when we arrived at the station.
examples.chinese: 我們到達車站時，外面正下著大雨。

sentences.englishOriginal: "EEEW! What are you eating?" Buster asked.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「噁！你到底在吃什麼啊？」巴斯特問道。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: EEEW!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 感嘆詞（常拼作 ew 或 eeew），用來表達極度厭惡、噁心或反感的道地口語發聲。

examples.english: Ew! There is a dead bug on the table.
examples.chinese: 噁！桌上有一隻死掉的蟲。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's all green and slimy!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這東西看起來綠綠的，而且黏糊糊的！」

vocabularyNotes.title: slimy
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「黏糊糊的、黏滑的」，通常帶有令人不舒服的語感。

examples.english: The rocks near the river were wet and slimy.
examples.chinese: 河流附近的岩石又濕又黏滑。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's an olive, pickle and green bean sandwich," said Carla.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這是橄欖、酸黃瓜加四季豆三明治，」卡拉說。

vocabularyNotes.title: pickle
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「醃菜、酸黃瓜」。在美式英語中，如果單獨說 pickle，通常指醃酸黃瓜。

examples.english: I always ask for extra pickles in my burger.
examples.chinese: 我點漢堡時總是要求加額外的酸黃瓜。

sentences.englishOriginal: "I made it myself. Would you like some? I brought extra."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這是我自己做的。你想吃一點嗎？我帶了多餘的。」

grammarNotes.title: Reflexive Pronoun (反身代名詞 強調用法)
grammarNotes.explanation: 將 myself 放在句子後面，用來強調「主詞親自動手做某事」，在此處表示三明治是卡拉親手做的。

examples.english: Did you paint this beautiful picture yourself?
examples.chinese: 這幅美麗的畫是你自己畫的嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: "No way!" said Buster, pinching his nose. "That's gross!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「怎麼可能！」巴斯特捏著鼻子說：「那太噁心了！」

vocabularyNotes.title: gross
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「噁心的、令人作嘔的」，在美式校園或日常口語中極為常見。

examples.english: Don't pick your nose in public; it's gross.
examples.chinese: 不要在公共場合挖鼻子，那很噁心。

phraseNotes.title: No way!
phraseNotes.explanation: 道地的口語表達，表示「絕不可能、沒門」，用來強烈拒絕或表達不可置信。

examples.english: No way! I am not going into that haunted house.
examples.chinese: 門都沒有！我才不要進去那間鬼屋。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's not gross," said Carla. "It's different. I like to be different."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這才不噁心呢，」卡拉說：「這叫與眾不同。我就是喜歡很不一樣。」

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's not different," said Buster. "It's gross." And he went to sit next to Leslie instead.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這不叫與眾不同，」巴斯特說：「這叫噁心。」於是，他索性換到蕾絲莉旁邊坐。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: instead
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 副詞「反而、作為替代」，常放在句尾，表示放棄了原本的選項而改做另一件事。

examples.english: There was no coffee left, so I drank tea instead.
examples.chinese: 咖啡不剩了，所以我改喝茶。

sentences.englishOriginal: On Tuesday Carla's sandwich was long with something yellow and white oozing out the sides.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 到了星期二，卡拉帶的三明治變成長條形的，兩側還滲出黃黃白白的東西。

vocabularyNotes.title: ooze
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「滲出、冒出、緩慢流出」。

examples.english: Delicious melted cheese was oozing out of the warm pizza.
examples.chinese: 美味的融化起司正從溫熱的披薩中滲出來。

sentences.englishOriginal: "What in the world is that?" asked Leslie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這到底是天底下的什麼怪物啊？」蕾絲莉問道。

phraseNotes.title: what in the world
phraseNotes.explanation: 常見的強烈語氣片語，相當於「究竟、到底」，常用於疑問句中表示極度驚訝或困惑。

examples.english: What in the world happened to your new shoes?
examples.chinese: 你的新鞋子究竟發生了什麼事？

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's my Banana-Cottage-Cheese Delight," said Carla, "on a tasty toasted baguette."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這是我的『香蕉茅屋起司特調』，」卡拉說：「配上美味的烤法式長棍麵包。」

vocabularyNotes.title: delight
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞在此作「令人愉悅的食品/佳餚」，常在菜單或自創料理名稱中用作後綴。

examples.english: The chef's chocolate dessert is a true delight.
examples.chinese: 主廚的巧克力甜點真是一道人間美味。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Bananas and cottage cheese?" asked Leslie, sticking out her tongue. "That's disgusting."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「香蕉加茅屋起司？」蕾絲莉吐了吐舌頭問：「這太噁心了吧。」

vocabularyNotes.title: disgusting
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「令人噁心的、極差勁的」，語氣比 gross 更強烈一些。

examples.english: The trash smelled absolutely disgusting after three days.
examples.chinese: 垃圾放了三天之後，聞起來簡直噁心透頂。

phraseNotes.title: stick out one's tongue
phraseNotes.explanation: 「吐舌頭」，通常是小孩子做鬼臉或表達厭惡、調皮的動作。

examples.english: The little boy stuck out his tongue at his sister.
examples.chinese: 那個小男孩對他的姐姐吐了吐舌頭。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's not disgusting," said Carla. "It's creative."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這不叫噁心，」卡拉說：「這叫有創意。」

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's disgusting," said Leslie, and she went to sit next to Natie instead.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這就是噁心，」蕾絲莉說，隨後她也換去坐到內提旁邊了。

sentences.englishOriginal: On Wednesday Carla's sandwich was orange and brown and lumpy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 到了星期三，卡拉的三明治變得又橘又啡，還一塊一塊結成堆。

vocabularyNotes.title: lumpy
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「滿是硬塊的、粗糙不平的」，由 lump（塊狀物）衍生而來。

examples.english: The gravy was lumpy and didn't taste very good.
examples.chinese: 那肉汁結滿了硬塊，味道不怎麼好。

sentences.englishOriginal: It crunched when she bit into it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她一口咬下去的時候，還發出了清脆的咔嚓聲。

vocabularyNotes.title: crunch
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「發出碎裂聲地咬、嘎吱作響地咀嚼」。

examples.english: She loved to crunch on fresh carrots.
examples.chinese: 她喜歡嘎吱嘎吱地啃新鮮胡蘿蔔。

phraseNotes.title: bite into
phraseNotes.explanation: 「大口咬下、狠狠咬進去」某種食物。

examples.english: He bit into the juicy apple and smiled.
examples.chinese: 他一口咬下多汁的蘋果，露出了笑容。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Ugh!" said Natie, who was sitting next to her now. "What is that?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「呃啊！」此時坐在她旁邊的內提叫道：「那又是什麼鬼東西？」

grammarNotes.title: Non-restrictive Relative Clause (非限定關係子句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 由逗號隔開的「, who...」，用來對前方的專有名詞 Natie 提供額外的補充說明，而不具限制特定人的功能。

examples.english: My uncle, who lives in New York, is a talented cook.
examples.chinese: 我的伯伯（他住在紐約）是一位非常有才華的廚師。

sentences.englishOriginal: "I call it Carla's Crunch," said Carla. "It's peanut butter, crackers and cheddar cheese in a lovely pita bread. I brought extra. Would you like some?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我叫它『卡拉酥脆號』，」卡拉說：「裡面包了花生醬、餅乾還有切達起司，裹在精緻的口袋餅裡。我有多帶，你想來點嗎？」

vocabularyNotes.title: pita
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「皮塔餅、口袋餅」，一種中東傳統的圓形口袋狀麵包。

examples.english: We stuffed the pita bread with grilled meat and salad.
examples.chinese: 我們在口袋餅裡塞滿了烤肉和沙拉。

sentences.englishOriginal: "No way!" said Natie, scrunching his face. "That's sick!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「才不要！」內提皺起眉頭苦著臉說：「這太變態了！」

vocabularyNotes.title: scrunch
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞在此指「擠壓、使皺縮」。scrunch one's face 意指因為反感或痛苦而把臉部肌肉擠皺在一起。

examples.english: The sour lemon made the little girl scrunch up her face.
examples.chinese: 酸溜溜的檸檬讓小女孩整張臉都皺了起來。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: That's sick!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 在美式俚語中，sick 除了生病，常在口語中用作表示「令人感到噁心、瘋狂、變態」或反常得難以接受。

examples.english: You put mayo on your watermelon? That's sick!
examples.chinese: 你在西瓜上加美乃滋？那太變態了吧！

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's not sick," said Carla. "It's unique."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這才不變態，」卡拉說：「這叫獨一無二。」

vocabularyNotes.title: unique
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「獨特的、獨一無二的」。

examples.english: Everyone's fingerprints are completely unique.
examples.chinese: 每個人的指紋都是完全獨一無二的。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's sick," said Natie. And he went to sit next to Marcus instead.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這就是變態，」內提說。於是，他也跑去坐在馬可斯旁邊了。

sentences.englishOriginal: On Thursday Carla brought a chopped liver, potato chip and cucumber sandwich.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 星期四的時候，卡拉帶了剁碎的肝臟、洋芋片配小黃瓜三明治。

sentences.englishOriginal: On Friday she brought a sardine and mustard sandwich with sunflower seeds.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 星期五的時候，她居然帶了沙丁魚加芥末，外加葵花子組成的三明治。

vocabularyNotes.title: sardine
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「沙丁魚」。

examples.english: The canned sardines were packed tightly in oil.
examples.chinese: 罐裝沙丁魚在油中緊密地排列著。

sentences.englishOriginal: By Monday, no one wanted to sit next to Carla, so she ate by herself.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 到了下個星期一，已經沒有任何同學願意坐在卡拉身邊了，所以她只能孤零零地自己吃午餐。

phraseNotes.title: by oneself
phraseNotes.explanation: 「獨自一人、靠自己地」，相當於 alone。

examples.english: She prefers traveling by herself to see the world.
examples.chinese: 她更喜歡獨自一人旅行去看看世界。

sentences.englishOriginal: At the end of the day, Miss Pimento made an announcement. "Tomorrow we will have a picnic."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 放學前，皮門托老師宣布了一件事：「明天我們要去野餐。」

vocabularyNotes.title: announcement
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「公告、宣布」。動詞形式為 announce。

examples.english: The principal made an important announcement over the loudspeaker.
examples.chinese: 校長透過廣播做了一個重要宣布。

phraseNotes.title: at the end of the day
phraseNotes.explanation: 字面上指「在一天即將結束之際/傍晚放學時」。在日常商業口語中亦常被引申作「歸根究底、到頭來」之意。

examples.english: At the end of the day, everyone packed their bags and went home.
examples.chinese: 一天的尾聲，大家都收拾好書包回家了。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Hurray!" everyone shouted. "A picnic! Yippee!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「萬歲！」全班興奮地大喊：「要去野餐了！耶比！」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Yippee!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 小孩子在極度高興或雀躍時發出的感嘆詞，類似於中文的「哇噢、太棒了」。

examples.english: Yippee! School is closed for winter break!
examples.chinese: 太棒了！學校開始放寒假了！

sentences.englishOriginal: The next day, when the lunch bell rang, the kids ran to get their picnic lunches.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隔天，當午餐鐘聲一響，所有小朋友便爭先恐後地跑去拿自己的野餐便當。

sentences.englishOriginal: "I have peanut butter and jelly," Natie announced to the class. "I have baloney," said Leslie. "Tuna," called Buster.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我帶的是花生醬加果醬三明治！」內提向全班宣告。「我的是波洛尼亞大紅腸，」蕾絲莉說。「鮪魚！」巴斯特大喊道。

vocabularyNotes.title: baloney
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「波洛尼亞香腸（一種常見的大熏腸）」，口語中也常用來胡謅、瞎說（Nonsense）。

examples.english: He packed a simple baloney sandwich for lunch.
examples.chinese: 他打包了一個簡單的大紅腸三明治當午餐。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Hey Carla, what do you have?" Carla didn't answer.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「嘿卡拉，妳帶了什麼？」卡拉沒有回答。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's probably a ketchup, spinach and jelly bean sandwich," joked Buster. He and Leslie howled. So did Natie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「該不會是番茄醬、菠菜配軟軟的果汁軟糖三明治吧，」巴斯特開玩笑說。他和蕾絲莉隨即放聲大笑，內提也跟著哄堂大笑。

vocabularyNotes.title: howl
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞原意為「野獸嚎叫」，在此借用為「因極度被逗樂而大笑、放聲大笑」。

examples.english: The comedians made the entire audience howl with laughter.
examples.chinese: 喜劇演員們讓全場觀眾放聲大笑。

grammarNotes.title: So + Auxiliary/Be Verb + Subject (倒裝句 也是如此)
grammarNotes.explanation: 當要表示「某人也做了同樣的事」時，可以用「So + 助動詞/be動詞 + 主詞」的結構。這裡的 did 代替了前面的動詞 howled。

examples.english: She passed the difficult test, and so did her brother.
examples.chinese: 她通過了那場難考的測試，她的哥哥也是如此。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It is not!" said Carla.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「才不是呢！」卡拉反駁。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: It is not!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 帶有強烈情緒的道地反駁用語，故意重讀 is，用來加強語氣全盤否定對方的猜測。

examples.english: You're afraid of the dark! — I am not!
examples.chinese: 你怕黑！——我才沒有！

sentences.englishOriginal: "Let's have some quiet in here," said Miss Pimento, "or we won't be able to have our picnic." The class was suddenly silent.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「大家都安靜一下，」皮門托老師說：「否則我們就不能去野餐了。」教室裡頓時變得鴉雀無聲。

phraseNotes.title: be able to
phraseNotes.explanation: 「能夠……、有能力……」，常接在情態助動詞（如 won't）後面作為 can 的替代形式。

examples.english: With enough practice, you will be able to speak English fluently.
examples.chinese: 只要有足夠的練習，你就能夠流利地說英語。

sentences.englishOriginal: Then the children followed Miss Pimento, two by two, down the hall out the door and down the block to the park.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 接著，孩子們排成兩兩一隊，跟在皮門托老師後面，穿過走廊、走出大門，沿著街區一路走到了公園。

phraseNotes.title: two by two
phraseNotes.explanation: 「成雙成對地、兩個兩個一排地」，常用來形容學生排隊時兩人一組的狀態。

examples.english: The students marched into the museum two by two.
examples.chinese: 學生們兩個兩個一排地排隊走進博物館。

sentences.englishOriginal: "All right, everyone," said Miss Pimento. "Find a place to sit and bon appètit!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「好了，各位，」皮門托老師說：「自己找個位子坐下來，祝大家胃口大開！」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: bon appètit
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 源自法文的外來語，在英語世界中被廣泛借用，意思是「祝你胃口好、用餐愉快」，常用在開動前的祝福語。

examples.english: Dinner is served. Bon appétit, everyone!
examples.chinese: 晚餐準備好了。大家用餐愉快！

sentences.englishOriginal: Carla took a bite of her sandwich.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 卡拉咬了一口她的三明治。

examples.english: He took a small bite of the chocolate cake.
examples.chinese: 他咬了一小口巧克力蛋糕。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Yuck!" said Buster, pointing at Carla's sandwich. "What is that -- a worm sandwich?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「呸！」巴斯特指著卡拉的三明治大叫：「那又是什麼——毛毛蟲三明治嗎？」

phraseNotes.title: point at
phraseNotes.explanation: 「用手指著……」，通常帶有直接、甚至有時不夠禮貌的動作語感。

examples.english: It is rude to point at other people in public.
examples.chinese: 在公共場合用手指著別人是不禮貌的。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Yuck!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 經典的美式感嘆詞，用來表達對食物或事物極其厭惡、覺得噁心，相當於中文的「噁、呸」。

examples.english: Yuck! This milk has gone sour.
examples.chinese: 呸！這牛奶已經酸掉了。

sentences.englishOriginal: "For your information," said Carla, "It's a lettuce, tomato, raisin, bean sprout, pretzel and mayonnaise sandwich. I call it the Combo Deluxe."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「實話告訴你吧，」卡拉不甘示弱地說：「這可是集結了萵苣、番茄、葡萄乾、豆芽菜、蝴蝶脆餅再搭配美乃滋的三明治。我叫它『豪華全套號』。」

vocabularyNotes.title: pretzel
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「椒鹽捲餅、蝴蝶脆餅」，一種常帶有鹽粒、形狀獨特的脆餅。

examples.english: He bought a large, warm soft pretzel at the mall.
examples.chinese: 他在購物中心買了一個大大的、熱騰騰的軟椒鹽捲餅。

phraseNotes.title: for your information
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用口語縮寫為 FYI。在對話中單獨使用時，常帶有一種「實話告訴你、坦白說（略帶反駁或高傲）」的語氣，用來向對方澄清事實。

examples.english: For your information, I finished that project yesterday.
examples.chinese: 實話告訴你吧，我昨天就已經把那個專案做完了。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It looks more like a Wormbo Deluxe!" teased Buster. Leslie and Natie burst out laughing.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這看起來更像是『豪華毛蟲號』吧！」巴斯特嘲笑道。蕾絲莉和內提立刻爆發出大笑聲。

vocabularyNotes.title: tease
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「取笑、戲弄、開玩笑」。

examples.english: The older boys used to tease him about his glasses.
examples.chinese: 以前那些大男孩常拿他的眼鏡來取笑他。

phraseNotes.title: burst out laughing
phraseNotes.explanation: 極為道地的固定片語，表示「突然放聲大笑、大笑出聲」。後面一定要接 V-ing 形式。

examples.english: When the clown fell down, the children burst out laughing.
examples.chinese: 當小丑摔倒時，孩子們突然放聲大笑。

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster rummaged through his knapsack. "Uh oh," he said quietly. He rummaged some more. "Uh oh," he said again.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特在背包裡翻來翻去。「呃哦，」他小聲嘟噥。他又翻了一會兒。「完了，」他再次低聲說道。

vocabularyNotes.title: rummage
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「翻找、搜尋（通常指翻得亂七八糟的尋找）」。

examples.english: She rummaged through her drawer looking for the missing keys.
examples.chinese: 她翻遍了抽屜，尋找那把弄丟的鑰匙。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Uh oh
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中非常高頻的感嘆詞，用來表示意識到事情搞砸了、出狀況、有麻煩了。

examples.english: Uh oh, I think I left the stove running.
examples.chinese: 糟糕，我好像忘了關瓦斯爐。

sentences.englishOriginal: He dumped everything out of his bag. "I can't believe it," Buster said sadly. "I forgot my sandwich."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他把包包裡所有的東西都倒了出來。「我真的不敢相信，」巴斯特沮喪地說：「我竟然忘了帶我的三明治。」

vocabularyNotes.title: dump
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「傾倒、一下子倒出來」。

examples.english: He dumped his dirty clothes straight into the washing machine.
examples.chinese: 他把髒衣服直接一股腦倒進了洗衣機。

phraseNotes.title: I can't believe it
phraseNotes.explanation: 「我真不敢相信」，用來抒發強烈的震驚、無奈或失望之情。

examples.english: I can't believe it! We won the first prize!
examples.chinese: 我真不敢相信！我們竟然得了第一名！

sentences.englishOriginal: "That's awful," said Leslie, biting into her baloney sandwich. "A real bummer," said Natie, chomping on his peanut butter and jelly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「那太慘了吧，」蕾絲莉一邊大口咬著她的大紅腸三明治一邊說。「真倒楣，」內提也跟著說，嘴裡還一邊大嚼著他的花生醬果醬三明治。

vocabularyNotes.title: chomp
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「大聲地咀嚼、大口地咬」，常接介系詞 on。

examples.english: The horse was happily chomping on the fresh grass.
examples.chinese: 馬兒正開心地大口大口嚼著鮮草。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: A real bummer
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 美式道地口語俚語，bummer 指「令人失望、無奈或倒楣的事」。A real bummer 就是「真是一件讓人沮喪/倒楣透頂的事」。

examples.english: The outdoor concert was canceled due to rain? That's a real bummer.
examples.chinese: 戶外音樂會因為下雨取消了？那太令人掃興了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Soon everyone was eating -- everyone except Buster.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不久，每個人都在開心地大快朵頤——除了巴斯特以外。

grammarNotes.title: Except as a Preposition (except 的介系詞用法)
grammarNotes.explanation: except 用作介系詞，表示「除了……之外（不包含在內）」，常置於被排除的名詞前方。

examples.english: The store is open every day except Sunday.
examples.chinese: 這間店除了星期天之外天天都有營業。

sentences.englishOriginal: Carla looked at Buster. She looked at her sandwich. She looked back at Buster.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 卡拉看了看巴斯特。她低頭看看自己的三明治。接著，又轉回來看著巴斯特。

sentences.englishOriginal: "You can have one of mine," she offered. "I brought extra."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我的可以分一個給你吃喔，」她主動提議：「我有另外多帶。」

grammarNotes.title: Possessive Pronoun (所有格代名詞)
grammarNotes.explanation: 使用 mine 代替前面提過的「my sandwiches」，避免字詞重複，使句子更精煉。

examples.english: Your coat is warm, but mine is waterproof.
examples.chinese: 你的外套很保暖，但我的這件是防水的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Some kids snickered. "No thanks," said Buster glumly. "I'm not that desperate."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 一旁有幾個小孩偷偷笑了起來。「免了，謝謝，」巴斯特悶悶不樂地說：「我也沒慘到那種地步。」

vocabularyNotes.title: snicker
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「竊笑、暗笑、吃吃地笑」，通常指帶有嘲諷意味的笑。

examples.english: They snickered behind his back when he made a mistake.
examples.chinese: 他犯錯的時候，他們在他背後偷偷竊笑。

vocabularyNotes.title: desperate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「絕望的、極度渴望的、鋌而走險的」。在此口語用法中表示「處境慘到飢不擇食的境地」。

examples.english: The lost travelers were desperate for food and water.
examples.chinese: 迷路的旅人極度渴望能獲得食物和水。

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster's mouth began to water. Buster looked at Carla's sandwich.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特此時開始瘋狂分泌唾液。他死死盯著卡拉的三明治。

phraseNotes.title: one's mouth begins to water
phraseNotes.explanation: 道地的英文片語，意思是「流口水、垂涎欲滴」，形容看到好吃的東西時肚子很餓的反應。

examples.english: The smell of freshly baked bread made his mouth water.
examples.chinese: 剛出爐的麵包香氣讓他直流口水。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Maybe bean sprouts aren't so bad," he thought.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「或許豆芽菜吃起來其實也沒那麼糟吧，」他暗自思忖。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It's really quite delicious," said Carla, catching Buster's glance. Buster quickly turned away.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這吃起來真的蠻好吃的喔，」卡拉捕捉到了巴斯特偷偷瞄過來的眼神，笑著說。巴斯特立刻心虛地把頭轉開。

phraseNotes.title: turn away
phraseNotes.explanation: 「轉過臉去、避開視線」。

examples.english: She turned away to hide her tears.
examples.chinese: 她把臉轉過去，好掩飾自己的眼淚。

grammarNotes.title: Present Participle as an Adverbial Modifier (現在分詞作伴隨狀語)
grammarNotes.explanation: 使用「, catching...」結構，表示這個動詞與主句的動作（said Carla）同時發生，用來豐富情節的畫面感。

examples.english: He walked out of the room, shutting the door quietly.
examples.chinese: 他一邊走出房間，一邊輕輕地把門關上。

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster was growing hungrier by the minute and his stomach growled loudly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隨著時間一分一秒過去，巴斯特覺得肚子越來越餓了，肚子還發出了好大一聲「咕嚕嚕」的慘叫。

vocabularyNotes.title: growl
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「（肚子）咕嚕叫、（野獸）低吼」。

examples.english: My stomach always growls right before lunchtime.
examples.chinese: 我的肚子總是在午餐時間前咕嚕咕嚕叫。

phraseNotes.title: by the minute
phraseNotes.explanation: 「每分鐘地、一分一秒地」，用來形容某種狀態變化得非常迅速且持續不斷。

examples.english: The weather was getting colder by the minute.
examples.chinese: 天氣一分一秒地變得越來越冷。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Raisins are kind of fun," he thought, "and who doesn't like pretzels?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「葡萄乾吃起來其實挺有意思的，」他想：「而且，有誰會不喜歡蝴蝶脆餅呢？」

phraseNotes.title: kind of
phraseNotes.explanation: 副詞片語「有一點、算是、稍微」，在口語中極常充當語氣緩和詞。

examples.english: I'm kind of tired after the long soccer game.
examples.chinese: 踢完漫長的足球賽後，我有一點累了。

grammarNotes.title: Rhetorical Question (反問句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「who doesn't like...?」形式上是個否定疑問句，實質上是用來強烈肯定「每個人都喜歡蝴蝶脆餅」。

examples.english: Who doesn't want to be happy?
examples.chinese: 有誰會不想快樂呢？（意即每個人都想快樂）

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster eyed Carla's sandwich again. "You don't know what you're missing..." Carla sang out.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特忍不住又嫖了卡拉的三明治一眼。「你根本不知道自己錯過了什麼人間美味喔……」卡拉故意拉長尾音、像唱歌一樣俏皮地調侃道。

vocabularyNotes.title: eye
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「打量、注視、盯著看」，含有密切關注或渴望的意味。

examples.english: The cat eyed the goldfish swimming in the bowl.
examples.chinese: 那隻貓死死盯著在碗裡游動的金魚。

phraseNotes.title: miss out
phraseNotes.explanation: 「錯過（好機會、好東西）」。這裡的 you don't know what you're missing 是一個經典的日常美式口語套話，意思是「你不知道你錯過了什麼好康/美味」。

examples.english: You should come to the party, or you'll miss out on the fun.
examples.chinese: 你應該來參加派對，不然你就會錯過有趣的活動。

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster couldn't take it anymore. He looked around. Everyone was busy eating. No one was watching him.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特終於忍無可忍了。他環顧四周。同學們都正忙著埋頭大吃，根本沒有人在注意他。

phraseNotes.title: can't take it anymore
phraseNotes.explanation: 高頻日常口語，意思是「再也受不了了、無法再忍受了」。

examples.english: The noise was so loud that I couldn't take it anymore.
examples.chinese: 噪音實在太大了，我再也受不終了。

phraseNotes.title: be busy doing something
phraseNotes.explanation: 「忙著做某事」，後面接名詞或現在分詞（V-ing），中間省略了介系詞 in。

examples.english: The kids were busy preparing for the Halloween party.
examples.chinese: 孩子們正忙著準備萬聖節派對。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Okay," he whispered to Carla. "Okay, what?" asked Carla.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「好啦，」他小小聲地對卡拉說。「好啦是什麼意思？」卡拉故意問道。

vocabularyNotes.title: whisper
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「低語、耳語、小聲說話」。

examples.english: She leaned in to whisper a secret to her best friend.
examples.chinese: 她湊過去跟她最好的朋友低聲說了一個秘密。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Okay, can I have one?" he whispered again. "Can you have one what?" asked Carla.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「好吧，我可以拿一個嗎？」他再度壓低聲音。「你可以拿一個什麼啊？」卡拉繼續逗他。

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster blurted impatiently, "Can I please have one of your sandwiches?" Everyone looked up.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特終於按捺不住，不耐煩地脫口大喊：「請問我可以吃一個妳的三明治嗎？」這一下，全班同學都猛地抬起頭來。

vocabularyNotes.title: blurt
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「不經思考地脫口而出、衝口地說」，通常後接說出的內容，或與 out 連用。

examples.english: He was so nervous that he blurted out the secret.
examples.chinese: 他太緊張了，以至於不小心把秘密脫口說了出來。

phraseNotes.title: look up
phraseNotes.explanation: 「抬頭看、向上看」。

examples.english: They looked up at the stars twinkling in the dark sky.
examples.chinese: 他們抬頭看著黑夜中閃爍的星星。

sentences.englishOriginal: Carla smiled and handed Buster a Combo Deluxe.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 卡拉微微一笑，大方地遞給巴斯特一個「豪華全套號」。

phraseNotes.title: hand someone something
phraseNotes.explanation: 「把某物遞給某人」，hand 在此作動詞用。

examples.english: Could you please hand me that dictionary on the shelf?
examples.chinese: 可以請你把書架上的那本字典遞給我嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster examined the lettuce, tomato, raisin, bean sprout, pretzel and mayonnaise sandwich carefully.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特一臉戒備、小心翼翼地端詳著這個夾著萵苣、番茄、葡萄乾、豆芽菜、蝴蝶脆餅和美乃滋的三明治。

vocabularyNotes.title: examine
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「仔細檢查、細心端詳、審視」。

examples.english: The doctor examined the patient's throat carefully.
examples.chinese: 醫生仔細檢查了病人的喉嚨。

sentences.englishOriginal: He looked at Leslie, then Natie, then Carla. And then he took a very small bite.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他看了看蕾絲莉，又看看內提，最後看看卡拉。接著，他終於咬了微乎其微的一小口。

sentences.englishOriginal: All eyes were watching as he chewed and swallowed.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在全班目不轉睛的注視下，他慢慢地咀嚼，然後吞了下去。

vocabularyNotes.title: swallow
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「吞嚥、嚥下」。

examples.english: Drink some water to help you swallow the pill.
examples.chinese: 喝點水好幫你把藥丸吞下去。

phraseNotes.title: all eyes
phraseNotes.explanation: 道地的借喻修辭，意思是「所有人的目光、全神貫注地注視」。例如 be all eyes 意即目不轉睛。

examples.english: When the actress walked onto the stage, all eyes were on her.
examples.chinese: 當女演員走上舞台時，所有人的目光都集中在她身上。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Well?" asked Leslie impatiently. "Well?" asked Natie. Buster didn't say anything.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「怎麼樣？」蕾絲莉急切地追問。「到底怎樣？」內提也跟著問。巴斯特什麼話也沒有說。

sentences.englishOriginal: He looked at everyone and took another bite. And another. And another.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他看了看大家，接著又狠狠咬了一大口。然後一小口接一小口，完全停不下來。

sentences.englishOriginal: "I can't believe he's eating it!" said Natie in disgust. "What does it taste like, Buster?" asked Leslie. "Is it gross?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我真不敢相信他居然真的吃下去了！」內提一臉嫌惡地說。「巴斯特，這到底是什麼味道啊？」蕾絲莉問：「真的很噁心嗎？」

phraseNotes.title: in disgust
phraseNotes.explanation: 「厭惡地、嫌惡地」，表示帶有噁心、反感的情緒狀態。

examples.english: She turned her head away in disgust when she saw the trash mountain.
examples.chinese: 看到那座垃圾山時，她嫌惡地把頭轉了過去。

phraseNotes.title: what does it taste like
phraseNotes.explanation: 詢問某樣食物「吃起來味道如何、像什麼味道」的標準句型。

examples.english: What does dragon fruit taste like? Is it sweet?
examples.chinese: 火龍果吃起來是什麼味道？很甜嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: Buster didn't answer. He was too busy eating.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巴斯特根本顧不上回答。他正忙著風捲殘雲般大吃特吃呢。

grammarNotes.title: too... to... (太……以至於不能…… 結構變體)
grammarNotes.explanation: 此處使用「too + 形容詞 + busy + V-ing」，表示巴斯特「太忙於吃東西了，以至於無法回答問題」。

examples.english: She was too tired to finish her homework last night.
examples.chinese: 她昨天晚上太累了，以至於沒辦法完成功課。

sentences.englishOriginal: When the last bite was gone, Buster licked his fingers and smacked his lips. "Yum!" he said. "That was the best sandwich I ever ate!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當最後一口三明治被消滅乾淨，巴斯特吮了吮手指，還吧唧吧唧地咂了咂嘴。「太美味了！」他說：「這是我這輩子吃過最棒的三明治！」

vocabularyNotes.title: smack
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞在此指「（吃得津津有味時）咂嘴、使勁作聲地合攏雙唇」。smack one's lips 通常是覺得食物非常好吃時的本能動作。

examples.english: The kids smacked their lips when the chocolate cake arrived.
examples.chinese: 當巧克力蛋糕送上桌時，孩子們一個個咂了咂嘴。

grammarNotes.title: Omission of Relative Pronoun (關係代名詞的省略)
grammarNotes.explanation: 在 the best sandwich 之後省略了關係代名詞 that 或 which。因為這個關係代名詞在定語子句 (I ever ate) 中充當及物動詞 ate 的賓語，所以可以省略。

examples.english: This is the most exciting movie I have ever seen.
examples.chinese: 這是我看過最刺激的電影。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Yum!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語感嘆詞（也可以說 yummy），小孩子常用來表達食物非常好吃、極為美味，相當於繁體中文的「好吃、美味、好讚」。

examples.english: Yum! These freshly baked cookies look great.
examples.chinese: 哇，好讚！這些剛烤好的餅乾看起來太棒了。

sentences.englishOriginal: "It was?" asked Natie in horror. "It was?" asked Leslie in dismay. "It was!" said Buster, smiling at Carla. Carla beamed.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「真的假的？」內提驚恐萬狀地問。「怎麼可能？」蕾絲莉也大失所望。 「真的！」巴斯特看著卡拉笑道。卡拉一聽，整個人高興得眉開眼笑。

vocabularyNotes.title: beam
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞在此指「眉開眼笑、高興得發亮、展露燦爛的笑容」。

examples.english: The proud mother beamed as her son walked up to receive the award.
examples.chinese: 當兒子走上前領獎時，那位驕傲的母親笑逐顏開。

phraseNotes.title: in horror
phraseNotes.explanation: 「恐懼地、驚恐地」。

examples.english: She screamed in horror when she saw a large spider in her bed.
examples.chinese: 當她看到床上有一隻大蜘蛛時，嚇得驚恐地尖叫起來。

phraseNotes.title: in dismay
phraseNotes.explanation: 「沮喪地、大失所望地、驚慌失措地」。

examples.english: They looked at the ruins of their house in dismay.
examples.chinese: 他們看著自己房子的廢墟，感到大失所望。

sentences.englishOriginal: "I bet you'd all enjoy the Combo Deluxe," said Carla. "Who'd like to try some?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我敢說你們一定也都會喜歡這款『豪華全套號』的，」卡拉說：「有誰想要吃吃看嗎？」

phraseNotes.title: I bet
phraseNotes.explanation: 日常口語套話，意思是「我敢說、我敢打包票、我敢肯定」，用來表達高度把握的推測。

examples.english: I bet it will rain tomorrow; look at those dark clouds.
examples.chinese: 我敢打包票明天一定會下雨，你看那些烏雲。

sentences.englishOriginal: Slowly, Leslie raised her hand. And then Natie raised his. Then Darcy, Susan, Rufus and Fabio. Soon all the kids had their hands in the air.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 慢吞吞地，蕾絲莉率先舉起了手。緊接著內提也把手舉了起來。隨後是達西、蘇珊、魯夫斯和法比歐。不一會兒，所有小朋友全都把手舉得高高的。

phraseNotes.title: raise one's hand
phraseNotes.explanation: 「舉手」，常用在課堂發言或表達贊同、自願參加某事時。

examples.english: If you know the answer to this question, please raise your hand.
examples.chinese: 如果你知道這個問題的答案，請舉手。

sentences.englishOriginal: Carla took her last sandwich, broke it into small pieces and handed them out to everyone.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 卡拉拿出最後一個三明治，把它掰成一小塊一小塊，然後一個個分發給每個人。

phraseNotes.title: break into
phraseNotes.explanation: 「把（某物）折斷/掰成……」。

examples.english: She broke the chocolate bar into small pieces to share with her friends.
examples.chinese: 她把巧克力棒掰成小塊，和朋友們一起分享。

phraseNotes.title: hand out
phraseNotes.explanation: 「分發、發放（某物給大眾）」。

examples.english: The teacher handed out the test papers to the students.
examples.chinese: 老師把試卷一一分發給學生們。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Wow!" said Leslie, tasting her piece. "This is terrific!" "Yeah!" Said Natie. "It tastes great!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「哇！」蕾絲莉品嚐了一小口大讚：「這簡直太棒了！」「對啊！」內提也隨聲附和：「味道好極了！」

vocabularyNotes.title: terrific
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「極好的、了不起的、棒極了的」，在現代美式英語中是非常正面的稱讚詞。

examples.english: You did a terrific job on the stage tonight!
examples.chinese: 你今晚在舞台上的表現棒極了！

sentences.englishOriginal: "Tomorrow I'm going to bring a creative sandwich too," said Leslie. "Maybe it'll be a mustard sandwich with baked beans and French fries. What do you think of that, Carla?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「明天我也要帶一個超級有創意的三明治，」蕾絲莉興致勃勃地說：「也許是芥末醬配焗豆再加炸薯條的三明治。卡拉，妳覺得這點子怎麼樣？」

phraseNotes.title: what do you think of...?
phraseNotes.explanation: 詢問對方「你覺得……怎麼樣？、你對……有何看法？」的常用口語句型。

examples.english: What do you think of my new haircut?
examples.chinese: 你覺得我的新髮型怎麼樣？

sentences.englishOriginal: "Sounds good," said Carla. "And it's definitely creative."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「聽起來很不賴啊，」卡拉回答：「而且這絕對算得上是創意滿分。」

vocabularyNotes.title: definitely
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞「毫無疑問地、肯定地、絕對地」。

examples.english: I will definitely come to your birthday party next week.
examples.chinese: 我下週絕對會去參加你的生日派對。

phraseNotes.title: sounds good
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語中回應對方提議時的極常用語，意為「聽起來不錯、好啊」，前面省略了主詞 It。

examples.english: Let's go watch a movie tonight. — Sounds good!
examples.chinese: 我們今晚去看電影吧。——聽起來不錯！

sentences.englishOriginal: "I'll bring a spaghetti and soy sauce sandwich," said Buster as he sat down next to Carla.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「那我明天要帶義大利麵加醬油三明治！」巴斯特一邊說著，一邊大喇喇地挨著卡拉坐了下來。

phraseNotes.title: sat down
phraseNotes.explanation: 「坐下來」（sit down 的過去式）。

examples.english: He sat down on the sofa and opened his book.
examples.chinese: 他在沙發上坐了下來，翻開了他的書。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Yum," said Carla and Leslie together. "I don't know what I'm bringing yet," said Natie, "but it'll be unique."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「聽起來好好吃喔，」卡拉和蕾絲莉異口同聲地說。「我還不知道我明天要帶什麼，」內提抓抓頭說：「不過，保證一定也會是獨一無二的奇特口味。」

phraseNotes.title: together
phraseNotes.explanation: 在此副詞作「共同地、一起、異口同聲地」。

examples.english: They sang the song together on the last day of school.
examples.chinese: 他們在學期的最後一天一起合唱了這首歌。

grammarNotes.title: Object Clause (賓語從句/目的從句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「what I'm bringing」是一個由疑問詞引導的從句，在主句中充當及物動詞短語 don't know 的賓語（受詞）。注意從句中要使用陳述句語序（主詞在動詞前）。

examples.english: I don't know where she went yesterday.
examples.chinese: 我不知道她昨天去哪裡了。

sentences.englishOriginal: The next day everyone in Miss Pimento's class brought an unusual sandwich to school.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 到了第二天，皮門托老師班上的每一個小朋友，真的都帶了稀奇古怪的三明治來上學。

vocabularyNotes.title: unusual
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞「不尋常的、獨特的、稀奇的」，由 usual 加否定前綴 un- 組成。

examples.english: It is quite unusual to see snow in this area during October.
examples.chinese: 十月份在這個地區看到下雪是相當罕見不尋常的。

sentences.englishOriginal: There was an asparagus and salad dressing sandwich, a pistachio and tangerine sandwich, and even a pizza sandwich.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 其中有蘆筍配沙拉醬三明治、開心果加橘子三明治，甚至還有人帶了披薩三明治。

vocabularyNotes.title: asparagus
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「蘆筍」。

examples.english: Grilled asparagus is one of my favorite side dishes.
examples.chinese: 烤蘆筍是我最喜歡的配菜之一。

vocabularyNotes.title: pistachio
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「開心果」。

examples.english: She bought a scoop of pistachio ice cream.
examples.chinese: 她買了一球開心果口味的冰淇餅。

sentences.englishOriginal: "What did you bring today, Carla?" asked Buster. "I'm not telling," said Carla. "You'll have to wait until lunch time."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「卡拉，妳今天帶了什麼口味啊？」巴斯特好奇地探頭問。「我才不告訴你呢，」卡拉神秘地說：「你非得等到午餐時間不可。」

phraseNotes.title: have to
phraseNotes.explanation: 「必須、不得不」，此處結合了未來式 will have to，表示「你將非得……不可」。

examples.english: You will have to clear the validation check before submitting the file.
examples.chinese: 在提交檔案之前，你將必須先通過校驗。

sentences.englishOriginal: The morning seemed to last forever, but finally the lunch bell rang.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這天早上似乎過得無比漫長，好不容易，午餐的鐘聲終於敲響了。

phraseNotes.title: last forever
phraseNotes.explanation: 「持續到永遠、彷彿沒有盡頭地漫長」，常在文學或日常中用來誇張地形容時間過得很慢。

examples.english: The long, boring meeting felt like it would last forever.
examples.chinese: 那場又長又無聊的會議讓人覺得彷彿永遠開不完似的。

sentences.englishOriginal: While Buster was munching away on his spaghetti and soy sauce sandwich, he glanced over at Carla.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當巴斯特正大口大口開心地嚼著他的義大利麵醬油三明治時，他忍不住把目光朝卡拉那邊嫖了過去。

vocabularyNotes.title: glance
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「匆匆一瞥、掃視」，常與 over at 或 at 連用。

examples.english: He glanced at his watch and realized he was late.
examples.chinese: 他匆匆看了一眼手錶，發現自己遲到了。

phraseNotes.title: munch away on
phraseNotes.explanation: 「津津有味地大嚼、一直咯吱咯吱地吃著某物」，away 在此帶有持續、痛快進行動作的意味。

examples.english: The rabbit was munching away on a big green leaf.
examples.chinese: 兔子正津津有味地大口啃著一片綠色的大葉子。

sentences.englishOriginal: This time her sandwich was not green. It was not slimy or lumpy and nothing was oozing out the sides.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這一次，她帶的三明治不是綠色的。既不顯得黏糊糊，也沒有結成硬塊，更沒有任何奇怪的東西從邊緣滲漏出來。

sentences.englishOriginal: "So, what kind of sandwich is that?" Buster asked. "Yeah, Carla," said Leslie. "What's inside?" Natie looked at Carla, waiting for an answer.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「所以說，那到底是哪一種三明治啊？」巴斯特忍不住發問。「對啊，卡拉，」蕾絲莉幫腔：「裡面到底包了什麼？」內提也目不轉睛地盯著卡拉，等待著答案。

phraseNotes.title: what kind of...?
phraseNotes.explanation: 詢問「哪一種類型的……？」的基礎日常句型。

examples.english: What kind of books do you usually like to read?
examples.chinese: 你平時通常喜歡讀哪一類型的書？

sentences.englishOriginal: "Well," said Carla. "Today I have... peanut butter and jelly."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這個嘛，」卡拉不緊不慢地說：「我今天帶的是……花生醬加果醬三明治。」

sentences.englishOriginal: "Peanut butter and jelly?" asked Buster in disbelief. "Peanut butter and jelly?" Leslie and Natie asked together.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「花生醬加果醬？！」巴斯特滿臉不可置信地大喊。「花生醬加果醬？」蕾絲莉和內提也異口同聲、驚掉了下巴似地追問。

vocabularyNotes.title: disbelief
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞「不相信、懷疑、懷疑不信的狀態」。in disbelief 意思是不可置信地。

examples.english: He shook his head in disbelief when his name was called.
examples.chinese: 當他的名字被叫到時，他不可置信地搖了搖頭。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Peanut butter and jelly," said Carla, biting into her sandwich. "I like to be different."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「就是花生醬加果醬，」卡拉舒舒服服地一口咬下她的三明治說：「我就是喜歡與眾不同。」

sentences.englishOriginal: The end.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 故事結束。

sentences.englishOriginal: I picked "Carla's Sandwich" because I like the idea of kids learning to celebrate their uniqueness.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我之所以挑選《卡拉的三明治》這本書，是因為我很喜歡「讓孩子們學會去讚美、擁抱自己的獨特性」這個很棒的想法。

grammarNotes.title: Adverbial Clause of Cause (原因狀語從句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 由 because 引導的從句，用來解釋朗讀者挑選這本童書的背後核心原因。

examples.english: I ordered a pizza because I didn't want to cook tonight.
examples.chinese: 我叫了披薩，因為我今晚不想做飯。

sentences.englishOriginal: The things that make you you are you. There's no other person like you in the world and that should be celebrated.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 正是那些特殊的特質，才塑造了無可取代的你。世界上再也沒有第二個跟你一模一樣的人了，這絕對是一件非常值得去慶賀和驕傲的事。

grammarNotes.title: Attributive Clause with "that" (that 引導的定語從句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「that make you you」是一個限定性關係子句，用來修飾前方的主詞名詞 components (things)，關係代名詞 that 在此充當子句的主詞。

examples.english: The books that are on the desk belong to the school library.
examples.chinese: 書桌上的那些書是屬於學校圖書館的。

sentences.englishOriginal: I think, at the end of the story, the kids learn to appreciate Carla's uniqueness and I love that part of the story.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我覺得在故事的最後，孩子們終於學會了去欣賞、尊重卡拉身上那份最寶貴的與眾不同，而這正是我最著迷、最喜歡這部作品的地方。

vocabularyNotes.title: appreciate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞「欣賞、感激、體會」。在此指同理並珍視他人的優點與特質。

examples.english: It takes time to appreciate the beauty of classical poetry.
examples.chinese: 欣賞古典詩詞的美是需要時間沉澱的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Thank you for watching Storyline Online. Make sure to check out all of our stories. Keep watching and keep reading.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 感謝您收看「線上故事剧场」。請一定要記得去看看我們其他的精彩故事喔。保持觀看，熱愛閱讀，我們下次見！

phraseNotes.title: make sure to
phraseNotes.explanation: 「務必、一定要記得去做某事」，帶有叮嚀或溫馨提示的語感。

examples.english: Please make sure to turn off the lights before you leave the room.
examples.chinese: 離開房間前請務必記得關燈。

phraseNotes.title: check out
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語中極高頻的常用短語，意思是「去看看、瞧瞧、去探索/點閱（有趣的內容）」。

examples.english: You should check out the new bakery that opened down the street.
examples.chinese: 你應該去瞧瞧街角新開的那家麵包店。

phraseNotes.title: keep doing something
phraseNotes.explanation: 「持續、保持做某個動作」，keep 後方必須直接連接現在分詞（V-ing）。

examples.english: Keep trying, and you will eventually reach your coding goals.
examples.chinese: 堅持不斷嘗試，你最終一定能實現你的程式設計目標。
